item.title: Gulp.
item.description: Edit: wow most viral, gonna use this opportunity to say I’m scheduled for thyroid cancer surgery end of the month and I’m heavily debating canceling my surgery altogether with the state of the world. My tumor is slow growing and hasn’t changed in my most recent 6 month ultrasound. Scared to be layed up in bed recovering with all this shite going on, or having to rely on medication to survive but maybe can’t get access to it. Dunno. Weird times. Be safe everyone
item.dc:creator: ProficientInGifs

item.title: Every morning and night I send my adult kids a handful of funny & uplifting memes, dad jokes, warnings about cats, advice, words of encouragement, a theme song of the day, and weird shit. Here’s 50 of the top of the sent pile. Enjoy.
item.description:
Best dad joke win ever. The kids get a major shot on National tv, and end up repeating your dad jokes.
Beware the cats
Beware the cats
Cats beware
Beware the cats
Beware the cats. They’ll trick you into renovating your home (like my kids’ cats are doing now).
Two of my kids are welders. They thought this was awesome.
Foxes are dogs with cat software. Beat cats at their own game.
You’ve got to constantly watch them. They’re always up to something.
I hope you feel like dancing with your friends today.
I hope you feel like being goofy with your friends today.
I hope you feel like dancing with your friends today.
This is true.
I have this mower. Luckily I have no moose. ?

item.title: CNN says the U.S. military is responsible for strike on the elementary school in Iran that killed OVER 165 children
item.description: The New York Times and Reuters have also reported the same conclusions and been chastised for propaganda by the Administration. Video evidence has surfaced along with the satellite images.
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: A day of celebration for me!
item.description: Long story short, got divorced in 2018; she cheated, lied about it, put a protective order on me, and I couldn't handle it. I hurt myself, things kept escalating, and I eventually took a plea deal for F3 with 36 months court probation, to reduce after completing probation. That 36 months completed in November, 2025 and I filed for the reduction in January, which was approved and signed in February. I went to court today to finalize the process and I am 100% no longer a felon! Now I can get a job without having to explain it; without risking being terminated due to it as I have in the past. Now life returns to normal. I'm so relieved. I'm gonna buy some liquor to celebrate.
item.dc:creator: nation543

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @tfoust10 drew this one-line drawing of a heart. Awesome. Check out their posts for other cool ones.
27 Man Tax: Channing Tatum
“Maybe all one can do is hope to end up with the right regrets.” - Arthur Miller So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. (Apparently doesn't work in firefox). ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me

item.title: Our country is run be corrupt idiots and pedos.
item.description: Trump said he refuses to sign any bill until the SAVE Act is passed. The veto power vested in the President by Article I, Section 7 of the Constitution has proven to be an effective tool in the executive branch's dealings with Congress. In order for a bill to become law, the President either signs the bill into law, or the President allows the bill to become law without signature after a 10-day period. https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/RS22188
item.dc:creator: Imadethisaccounttopost

item.title: You can give Twitch chat control over Elden Ring
item.description: Some of you guys mentioned you liked the behind the scenes etc, so thought I would share this since I got online today :) This was my first souls like game, I normally play horror or adventure games (Tomb Raider!!!!!) I enabled Crowd Control which means that you can set it so you pay money to receive "crowd coins" and your twitch chat can then use an extension on the stream to help or hinder a game. I did this and raised the money for Special Effect charity who make bespoke gaming setups for physically disabled people to improve their quality of life :)
item.dc:creator: MCroft07

item.title: How to recognise and test for AI-generated images
item.description:
This post is for @Viscouse, because they asked, and anyone else who is curious. I'll also do a post about AI-generated videos at some point. Unfortunately, a lot of the tips I'm going to give don't hold much water anymore or won't for much longer, simply because AI image generation is improving so fast. So my first and foremost tip is 1) Use a GOOD detector
If you've heard people saying "my art comes back as AI generated" - it's either because their art *is* AI generated - or edited using AI tools - or because they use a shitty detector. I spent a good two hours this morning checking various detectors and reading reviews and ratings. Many (most?) free AI detectors suck. Alas. Perhaps they were good at one point, but with AI image generators getting better, the detectors also need to improve to stay on top of the game. Currently, by far the best free, no-registration-required image detector is Hive Moderation's: https://hivemoderation.com/ai-generated-content-detection This isn't just my opinion, it keeps ranking highly when tech sites test detectors, too: https://usefulai.com/tools/ai-image-detectors https://www.makeuseof.com/ai-image-detectors-accuracy-test/ https://ai.tenorshare.com/detect-images-tips/best-ai-image-detectors.html I have used it and tested it for some time now and I have not seen it fail yet. Sidenote: Google Gemini has an integrated SynthID Watermark detector, which can detect images that have been generated by Google AI, but it cannot detect images from other generators. "You're using AI to test for AI? OMG. Loser!" AI works. For a lot of things. It's a technology that can be adapted for many different purposes. It can be a valuable tool in some very specific instances. One of my objections regarding AI is it's image generation side, because it was trained using stolen data. And because it is being used to replace artists. And because even here on Imgur, people are trying to scam users by advertising AI-generated picture books and art as their OC. This is why I think it is useful to recognise and point out AI generated images. If you don't want to use a detector, there are still other things to look out for: 2) When was the image originally posted? You can usually check this with a reverse image search. OpenAI released DALL-E 3 in October of 2023 and Midjourney released V6 in December of 2023. If the image is from before mid 2023 and it doesn't look obviously AI, it is, most likely, not AI. (Watch out with Google's Reverse Image search - it *does* give wrong dates for images posted on social media, especially Facebook. Go to the actual page it links and see if the image is there.) 3) The piss filter is sometimes still a thing: A yellow sheen is especially common with older versions of Open AI's image generation services.
I've seen it suggested that this yellow sheen is because some AI models were trained on images taken during the "golden hour" (sunrise/sunset) or images with soft evening lighting/light from a fire. 4. World inconsistency (aka "could this actually be true?")
This was posted as a photo of the inside of Fingal's Cave in Scotland. My alarm bells went off and I looked up what the cave actually looks like:
This photo is from https://www.philandgarth.com/isle-of-staffa-and-lunga-scotland/ More world inconsistency:
Would someone be allowed to wear that to court (the front will have her pubic area on show)? Are officers allowed to take photos of defendants? We can also use this "photo" as a good example of: 5. Logical inconsistency. Garbled text in the seal; angle and contents of photo on officer's phone not accurate. @FiftyShadesOfBroccoli also pointed out "Plus weird random folds on the skirt. Also, the flag starts with a yellow stripe at the bottom (which should be red) and only has 5 red stripes, or 6 tops if part of the flag is folded back (should be 7). And the white stripes are wider than the red ones". The more complicated a scene is, the more chance there is for the world not to make sense. This is true for video, too. This is easier to detect where there is a series of photos, which is very common with influencers and thirst accounts. Or for posts where the prompt gave more than one option and the poster thought "sure, let's use both".
The OP here claimed that the images are being detected as AI generated because she uses Gimp to blur out her tattoos. But Gimp doesn't magically change the backs of your chairs into different chairs. More logical inconsistency:
Here you can see the pattern in the necklace/collar not being quite symmetrical. One of the snakes doesn't have a split tongue (also: piss filter alert!). Yes, artists also make mistakes, but they don't usually make the same mistakes as AI. Especially if it is digital art, it is much easier to fix mistakes and less likely for obvious ones such as mentioned above to exist in a published image. Almost done! 6. Prompt bleed.
Often elements from one part of a prompt unintentionally influence another, causing a mixed output. You can bet your shiny hiney that the prompt for this included the words "cedar-stewed rabbit". The AI got confused and placed the dishes on (presumably) cedar stumps - no doubt also thinking that looked more indigenous. (This could probably fit with the "world inconsistency" above.) Even if you don't trust detectors, you could look up the real things mentioned here and see that it actually looks different in reality:
7. Check the source. You can usually track the source of an image down by using reverse image search or if there is a creator's watermark. Sometimes these accounts mention in their description that they use AI generated images. Sometimes they don't, but it is obvious by looking at their post history that they use it a lot. Sometimes a Google Gemini or other watermark is still available in the original image, which has since been cropped out before being shared further. Racking my brain, but I think the only tip I have left is the most frustrating one: 8. It looks like AI.
Have you noticed that American shows and British shows often look different? It's hard to put into words sometimes. Photos from different eras. Videos from commercials? Doorbell cams? Dash cams? Things often have a certain "look". This is often true of AI generated images as well. And I've noticed that these LLMs all have a slightly different style - MidJourney is the artsy one, NanoBanana Pro will give you photorealistic images, ChatGPT is the one that wants to sit at the table with the cool kids. There are other generators, too. But these are the ones I see that are most commonly used here. Once you get used to the various styles, it becomes easier to spot. It's hard to explain. I suspect it becomes a type of pattern recognition. But it cannot be considered a valid defense, especially since so many people shout "That's AI!" without knowing what they are talking about. But hopefully, with the tips above (and perhaps more some kind souls will leave in the comments), we can get better at correctly identifying and pointing it out. I also recommend https://www.riddance.ai/, which is a media literacy blog aimed at non-techy people who want to stay informed about AI video/image generation. The author, Jeremy Carrasco, also posts shorts about identifying AI generated media on Youtube and Instagram with the account @showtoolsai. If people have other recommendations, I'd love to hear them. (Sidenote: All the AI images in this post I got from posts from Imgur.) #ai #til
item.dc:creator: DutchBoeremeisie

item.dc:creator: theoxfordcomma

item.title: The Mighty Colbert
item.description: The Mighty Colbert The time has arrived for us all to prepare for the doleful departure of Stephen Colbert. How will we last in the gaping black hole that's left in the absence of this merry soul? For a decade of late night and nine years before of satirical japes on the Colbert rapport, we'd been lavished with laughter for 20 straight years from the genius between Stephen's two mismatched ears. Why does his exit stir up such despair? What mysterious magic imbues late night's air? Stephen's wit is the source of his lunatic art, his intelligence, wisdom, compassion, and heart. These are the virtues that bring out the best in even his gravest, most humorless guest. His monologues, though, with their slap-happy quirks, have clearly been Stephen's sublime masterworks. Each one is a targeted rotten tomato, bookended by music from Batiste and Cato. So why is he cancelled? Why trash all that pleasure? Why yank off the air this beloved national treasure? Stephen's tale is a lesson for all who come after. Beware of a boss with thin skin and no laughter. But don't mourn for Stephen. He's going to be fine. He'll only get better like aging fine wine. With a talent so rich and discernment so rare, there's much more to come from the mighty Colbert. -John Lithgow
item.dc:creator: beastiebunny

item.title: Wednesday Movie Meeeems
item.description:
Nothing to see here, just Tim Curry, David Hyde Pierce and Hank Azaria in the original Broadway cast of ‘Spamalot’ (2005)

item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: 4 computers, 535 people: EPSTEIN FILES
item.description: Users: All 535 members of the U.S. Congress (House and Senate) have the legal right to view the unredacted files. Terminals: There are only 4 computers designated for this task. Location: These computers are not in the Capitol; they are in a secure "reading room" at a Department of Justice (DOJ) annex. The Hours: Members can only access them during standard office hours (roughly 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. ET), and they must provide at least 24 hours' notice before their visit. ---------------- CONTEXT: ---------------- 3min video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1p8brXyHghw 50min video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ylvTFvJvB84 10min clips: https://www.justice.gov/epstein/files/DataSet%2010/EFTA01827430.pdf Evidence document EFTA01827430: https://www.justice.gov/epstein/files/DataSet%2010/EFTA01827430.pdf
item.dc:creator: originalmaja

item.title: You get what you pay for. Womp Womp, Sad Trombone, but it *IS* mystifying. WHY WOULD KAMALA SEND US TO WAR???
item.description: ALT-TEXT: Skinner out of touch meme template with the top reading: Is it possible that I am a low-information voter that was manipulated by corrupt politicians and a billionaire owned media apparatus that had me vote against my own self interests, because I trusted them to understand I didn't like institutions that invalidated my own worldview devoid of evidentiary proof, facts or evidence, so that I am a part of what has failed us, and I am somewhat to blame for casting my vote for a dishonest man who has never once been trustworthy? the bottom reads: NO. TRUMP BETRAYED ME.
item.dc:creator: unclefishbits
